block_00: Dominican Republic, New Zealand, and Saudi Arabia
block_03: In the previous letter, we talked about the digital marketing landscape of Thailand, Brazil, and the UAE. Today, we will share more country cards with advertising tips — this time, for the Dominican Republic, New Zealand, and Saudi Arabia.
block_09: The Dominican Republic boasts a rapidly growing online consumption. One positive outcome of the COVID-19 crisis is the incredible development of e-solutions and a higher interest in ways of making money online. Now, at the stage of development, it is a perfect time to enter this market.
block_10: Show the platform. Dominicans prefer seeing the product. Use images of the platform in creatives and make video tutorials so that users could clearly visualize their workflow.
block_11: Mention support. Local users are accustomed to reliable after-sales service, so it's a good idea to tell them that IQ Option offers 24/7 support in Spanish.
block_12: Partner with influencers. Dominicans rely on networking and personal relationships. They are more likely to listen to a popular blogger’s opinion than to an abstract ad.
block_13: Have your DNS resolve inside the Dominican Republic. If you register your domain from outside the Republic, requests for your domain will need to constantly enter and exit the national network, slowing down your website speed. To fix that, either register your domain with a registry in the Dominican Republic or use a cloud DNS service like
block_19: YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Pinterest, TikTok
block_20: The country ranks high in terms of economic development and technology, which makes New Zealand a land ripe with digital marketing opportunities.
block_21: Mind ad blockers. Half of the NZ population use them, which might equate to some resistance to online advertising. You should devise more targeted strategies and diversify your ad channels to address this setback.
block_22: Embrace diversity. Use ads depicting people of different appearance, skin color, gender, and age, since New Zealand is a very multinational and multicultural country.
block_23: Use emojis. 87% of NZ businesses use emojis in social posts, and local audience ❤️ them.
block_29: Saudi Arabia is a wealthy country with a high demand for online services. Although Saudi is a conservative society following the traditions of Islam, the high digital literacy of the population and the ubiquitous use of VPN services provide excellent digital marketing opportunities.
block_30: Respect the law. Saudi Arabia is a religious country with lots of sensitive topics. Exercise a healthy amount of situational awareness and don’t be overly insistent in your adverts. Make sure your texts and visuals don’t contain sensitive materials and follow the law, both Islamic and state.
block_31: Advertise Islamic accounts. Islam regards any interest as usury and forbids it. Islamic accounts allow trading without swaps and other commissions, that is, they are considered halal.
block_32: Mind the language. Almost a third of the residents of KSA are expatriates from other countries like Pakistan, India, the Philippines, and Arab countries like Egypt. Try to A-B test your creatives in English and the alternative languages and see what brings better results.
block_33: Thanks for following us through the series!
